danger_signals.visual_form: Mythical creatures from Greek and Arab mythology appearing in environment
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities that deal damage; indicates unsafe zones requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark castle environments, lair aesthetic, oppressive architectural design
danger_signals.meaning: Global threat condition where evil overlords control territory; world is in danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Princess Rosebud held captive in Blackhorn's castle; trap mechanisms in castle
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to critical NPCs; danger zones contain environmental hazards

danger_signals.visual_form: Blackhorn (the evil wizard) as boss entity in final lair
danger_signals.meaning: Boss-level threat; highest danger concentration at specific location

safety_signals.visual_form: Fairy character appears in certain stages offering aid
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction opportunity; provides weapon/magic restoration services

safety_signals.visual_form: Destroyable stone pillars yielding crystals, jewels, arrowheads, shields
safety_signals.meaning: Resource abundance indicates manageable zone; player can fortify position

safety_signals.visual_form: Red jewels, potions, extra lives as drop rewards
safety_signals.meaning: Recovery opportunity; hazard survivability has improved

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue armor (Roche in arcade); axe-wielding protagonist
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fire Axe (mythical/divine weapon); legendary Bash axe
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red armor palette swap represents second player (still heroic)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Divine blessing origin; heavenly revelation; royal kingdom of Remlia aesthetics

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Castle of Algerine (arcade) / Blackhorn's castle (NES); demon overlord sigil
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mythical creature enemies (Greek and Arab mythology); Blackhorn as dark wizard variant
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Demon-ruled world; dark castle fortress; oppressive lair; evil dimension aesthetics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six-stage progression through demon-controlled territory (forest → cave → lake → lift → castle → final battle); dark atmospheric design; mythological enemy creature types
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs tremble with fear; no leadership resistance until hero obtains divine axe; world requires external salvation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two-world aesthetic: Greenview High School (Earth reality) contrasts with Kingdom of Remlia (fantasy dimension); castle imprisonment imagery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player transported involuntarily; Cutie provides guidance; Rosebud held captive; Blackhorn revealed as dark alternate self

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cutie reborn as human; vision of Rosebud appears; embrace scene signals peace return; dream-reality ambiguity resolved positively
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Return to Earth possible; NPC relationships restored; cycle of threat broken

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Axe weapon power gauge fills automatically; fills faster with wing power-up; weapon upgrades (axe → spear → sword change visual form)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stronger attacks available after resource accumulation; player agency increases with item collection

environmental_storytelling.example: Six stages arranged forest → cave → lake → lift → castle → final battle creates visual narrative of approach to evil stronghold; progression through terrain types signals deepening danger
Castle of Algerine / Blackhorn's castle serves as visual seat of evil power; fortress design communicates centralized threat; castle aesthetic distinct from natural stages
Princess Rosebud captivity in castle makes stakes spatially explicit; player must navigate toward known captive location; trap mechanisms in castle communicate active danger
Axe → spear → sword visual upgrades represent increasing heroic power; weapon appearance reflects combat capability tier
Roche's receipt of Fire Axe from God establishes celestial sanction; mythical/legendary weapon aesthetic distinguishes hero's tool from common arms
Greek and Arab mythological creature enemies populate stages; creature selection communicates fantasy world genre and thematic sources; demon enemy types reinforce demon-ruled state

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Armed with Bash axe; magic available
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can attack, jump, cast spells; pauses game to switch weapons/magic; interacts with Cutie for upgrades

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Equipped with Fire Axe; power gauge active
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Automatically fills power gauge when not swinging; chooses fast weak swings vs. slow strong fire-axe attacks; uses thunder attack with charged arrowheads
Offers weapon changes and magic restoration; sacrifices life to allow Astyanax passage; reborn as human post-victory
Calls to Astyanax in dreams; warns of Blackhorn's true identity before final battle; rewards player with vision and teleportation home post-victory

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Final boss; evil alternate Astyanax; controls castle lair
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Holds Rosebud captive; sets castle traps; engages in final battle; revealed to be dimensional opposite of hero
Attack player on sight; drop power-up loot when destroyed; vary by stage type (forest enemies differ from cave enemies, etc.)
Oppresses population; controls world territory; serves as final confrontation target; forces hero's divine intervention need

notes: Astyanax demonstrates dual narrative structures between arcade and NES versions creating different world-state communications: arcade emphasizes divine hero saving demon-oppressed world through mythic combat; NES emphasizes interdimensional rescue quest with self-confrontation theme (evil alternate self). Visual progression through six distinct stage types (forest, cave, lake, lift, castle) communicates spatial approach to evil center. Weapon transformation system (axe → spear → sword) and magic gauge mechanics communicate player power growth as visual/mechanical manifestation. The game uses fantasy creature enemy types and dark castle architecture as primary danger signaling rather than explicit HUD warnings. NPC sacrifice (Cutie) and post-victory transformation (Cutie reborn) communicate narrative stakes through behavioral changes rather than static world visuals. Color-coded protagonist variants (blue Roche vs. red palette swap in arcade) maintain faction identity while enabling two-player cooperation.
